channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Twitter at 20: How we lost the public square
item.description: Twenty years ago, Jack Dorsey changed the world. He opened his phone and sent a message to a new platform he had created: “just setting up my twttr”. That post carries the ID 20.
item.dc:creator: Chris Stokel-Walker, Fast Company

item.title: Uganda reintroduces rhinos into a protected area where they have been extinct since 1983
item.description: Ugandan wildlife authorities reintroduced rhinos into a remote protected area where the large mammals once were poached into extinction.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Onen, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Drone video from inside a Fukushima reactor shows a hole in pressure vessel, likely fuel debris
item.description: A video taken by tiny drones sent into one of three damaged reactors at the Fukushima Daiichi nuclear power plant shows a gaping hole in the thick-walled steel container of the core, with lumps of likely fuel debris hanging from it, in a first sighting of a pressure vessel bottom since the meltdown
item.dc:creator: Mari Yamaguchi, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: OpenAI buys non-AI coding startup to help its AI to program
item.description: OpenAI on Thursday announced the acquisition of Astral, the developer of open source Python tools that include uv, Ruff and ty. It says that it plans to integrate them with Codex, its AI coding agent first released last year, as well as continuing to support the open source products.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Meteorite hunters scour Ohio for fragments of 7-ton space rock that crashed into Earth
item.description: Meteorite hunters are combing Ohio for fragments of a space rock that streaked across the sky earlier this week.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Aftoora-Orsagos And Mark Scolforo, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Traffic is dying as a media metric. What comes next is more important
item.description: Day by day there’s more evidence that AI is eating up the media world. A recent report from Growtika, a self-described SEO and AI search agency, analyzed data from the search analytics platform Ahrefs to show that traffic to many tech media sites is way down over the past couple of years.
item.dc:creator: Pete Pachal, Fast Company

item.title: OpenAI’s $50B AWS deal puts its Microsoft alliance to the test
item.description: Despite OpenAI’s multiple re-affirmations that its relationship with Microsoft is strong and central, in view of recent developments, Redmond doesn’t seem to be convinced.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Cato Networks unveils GPU-powered SASE with native AI security controls
item.description: Cato Networks this week launched two additions to its secure access service edge (SASE) platform that the company says will address security challenges enterprises are facing now: protecting the AI tools end users rely on, while also using AI to defend against sophisticated threats.
item.dc:creator: Network World
